The government's decision:
This includes providing MahaID to every beneficiary using the Mahasarathi portal.
The use of MahaID as a stand-alone sign-on (SSO) system is being mandated.
1. To provide MahaID to the citizens for availing various schemes and services of the Government of Maharashtra.
This MahaID is being made mandatory with effect from 1st May, 2026. For this
Use the website www.mahasarathi.maharashtra.gov.in.

Provisions of laws / regulations e.g. Aadhaar Act 2016, Digital Personal Data Protection (DPDP) Act
2023, the Information Technology Act 2000, etc. will remain in force.
4. To ensure effective and time-bound implementation of the Mahasarathy project, complete planning,

a) MahaSarathi Project Apex Committee
The composition of the Mahasarathy Project Summit Committee will be as follows: -

para_trans: Government Decision Number: Narrow-1725 / P.No.251 / Mothers
Powers and functions of the Mahasarathy Project Summit Committee: -
1) To formulate policy, develop systems and procedures for issuance of Maha ID numbers.
to make a decision.
2) Directing government agencies on data standards and procedures.
3) To approve the annual financial budget for the implementation of the Mahasarathi project.
4) Management of information of the Mahasarathi project, standards regarding the security of information, as well as other technical
Setting rules and procedures for safety measures.
5) To approve the establishment of a grievance redressal mechanism for citizens.
6) Other Committees, as required, to assist in the implementation of the Mahasarathy Project.
or appointing task forces.
7) Acquisition of assets required for the functioning of the Summit Committee.
8) Accepting aid or grants from various sources to carry out the functions of the Summit Committee.
9) Formulate procedures as required for effective implementation of the Mahasarathi project and
to improve it.
10) Nomination of nominated members of the Summit Committee.

b) MahaSarathi Executive Committee
The composition of the Mahasarathi Executive Committee shall be as follows: -
The office of the Executive Committee of the Mahasarathi: -
1) To provide comprehensive leadership, vision and direction for the implementation of the Mahasarathi project.
2) To plan, coordinate and monitor the Mahasarthi project, as well as the progress of the project from time to time.
to review.
3) To take strategic decisions, control the regulatory aspects of the Mahasarthi project and ensure the success of the project.
to guide the implementation.

e) MahaSarathi Project Implementation Committee
The composition of the Mahasarathy Project Implementation Committee will be as follows:
The mandate of the Mahasarathy Project Implementation Committee is: -
1) Authentication of schemes, services and benefits to government departments.
to make it.
2) Ensure that citizens' information is regularly updated.
(iii) the effective implementation of government policies, schemes, services, benefits or subsidies by the citizens;
Supervision of data analysis, etc.
